definition:
1. a half-boot , (Xenophon Historicus):—; κρηπῖδες soldiers' boots , i. e. soldiers  themselves, (Theocritus Poeta Bucolicus) 
2. generally, a groundwork, foundation, basement  of a temple or altar, (Herdotus Historicus), etc.:—;metaphorically, κρηπὶς σοφῶν ἐπέων (Pindar); οὐδέπω κρηπὶς κακῶν ὕπεστι we have not yet got to the bottom  of misery, (Aeschulus Tragicus); ἡ ἐγκράτεια ἀρετῆς κρηπίς self-control is the foundation  of virtue, (Xenophon Historicus) 
3. the walled edge  of a river, a quay , Lat. crepido, (Herdotus Historicus) (ML)
